title: Koeleria × simonkaii
intro: Koeleria × simonkaii is a hybrid of the Koeleria cristata, a species of grass in the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Koeleria × simonkaii has been used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria × simonkaii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Koeleria × simonkaii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and remedies for respiratory and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Koeleria × simonkaii is a hybrid species of grass within the genus Koeleria. It belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to certain regions. The classification of Koeleria × simonkaii also includes its kingdom as Plantae and the phylum as Liliopsida. Its order is Poales, and the class is Liliopsida. This grass species is further classified as an angiosperm, having flowers with true petals.
